name: CSA / MDA RADARSAT (fleet)

overview.value: The RADARSAT program is a fleet of three generations of Canadian Earth observation satellites using synthetic aperture radar (SAR) technology: RADARSAT-1, RADARSAT-2, and the RADARSAT Constellation Mission (RCM). RADARSAT-1 was Canada's first operational Earth observation satellite; RADARSAT-2 is operated commercially by MDA; the RCM consists of three identical satellites, and RCM data is used in maritime surveillance, ecosystem monitoring, and disaster relief efforts.

base.reason: Payload Space (trade press) states 'Canada has already launched five satellites across three missions through its Radarsat initiative'; no single CSA page states a combined program-wide total (CSA describes each generation separately)

Payload Space (trade press) states 'Radarsat-2 and the three imaging sats that comprise the Radarsat Constellation Mission (RCM) are still operational' (1 + 3 = 4); no single CSA page states this as one combined figure

eoPortal RADARSAT-1 mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states the SAR instrument 'used a single microwave frequency of 5.3 GHz in the C band'; all RADARSAT missions (RADARSAT-1/-2, RCM) operate in C-band, so the band is a fleet-wide fact; registry v2 EO spec backfill

generations.name: RADARSAT-1
generations.text: C-band SAR (5.3 GHz) in a 798 km sun-synchronous orbit; resolution and swath widths varied across modes from 8-100 m to 45-500 km, with a 24-day repeat cycle. Operational 1995-2013.

generations.name: RADARSAT-2
generations.text: C-band SAR (5.405 GHz) with fully polarimetric imaging modes; imaging resolution of 3-100 m across a 20-500 km ground swath, sun-synchronous orbit at 798 km. Commercially operated by MDA.

generations.name: RADARSAT Constellation Mission (RCM)
generations.text: Three-satellite C-band SAR constellation (launched 2019) with an imaging resolution of 3-100 m; provides a four-day exact revisit for coherent change detection and an average daily global re-look in medium 50 m resolution mode.

notes: Fleet parent for the Canadian RADARSAT program (RADARSAT-2, RCM). Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05). eoPortal EO spec backfill (2026-07-09): all RADARSAT missions operate in C-band (RADARSAT-1 page states 5.3 GHz C band), carried here as the fleet-wide spectral band. Per-satellite resolution and imaging modes differ across generations and are carried on the member profiles (radarsat-2, rcm), so resolution_m/swath_km/imaging_modes are left null on the fleet parent to avoid conflating heterogeneous figures. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-23): added sourced generations rows (RADARSAT-1, RADARSAT-2, RCM) from each mission's own eoPortal page instead of forcing a single fleet-wide resolution/swath figure, per the registry v2 generations mechanism; this profile's task-description slug guess ('radarsat-1') does not match this file (it is the umbrella fleet profile, not a RADARSAT-1-specific one), confirmed by its existing 'CSA / MDA RADARSAT (fleet)' name and by radarsat-2.json existing as a separate profile.

events.headline: RADARSAT-1 launches as Canada's first operational Earth observation satellite
RADARSAT-2 launches as Canada's commercially operated SAR successor
MDA acquires DigitalGlobe and rebrands as Maxar Technologies (per Via Satellite)
RADARSAT Constellation Mission's three satellites launch for MDA and the Canadian Space Agency
RADARSAT Constellation Mission's three satellites become fully operational
Northern Private Capital completes CAD 1 billion purchase of MDA from Maxar
